name: White Chocolate Ricotta-Filled Hazelnut-Chocolate Cookies
recipeIngredient: 4 oz Bittersweet chocolate, finely grated (about 1 cup)
40 Suzhou wrappers (Suzhou gow skins), see note
15 oz Ricotta cheese (about 2 Cups); room temperature
2 tsp Finely minced orange peel (orange skin only; no white pith or membrane)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a medium bowl, combine the hazelnuts and bittersweet chocolate; mix well.
3. Generously grease 2 heavy-gauge baking sheets with butter.
4. Arrange 9 to 12 Suzhou wrappers on each baking sheet, leaving approximately 3/4-inch between each wrapper.
5. Sprinkle about 1-1/2 tbsp of the hazelnut-chocolate mixture on each wrapper.
6. Bake for 9 or 10 minutes, rotating the pans from bottom to top and back to front.
7. Remove from oven and cool wrappers on baking racks.
8. Prepare the remaining wrappers the same way and bake until done.
9. Cool to room temperature.
10. In a medium bowl, combine the ricotta cheese, white chocolate, powdered sugar, and orange peel; mix well.
11. Using approximately 1 tbsp per cookie, center the ricotta mixture on the nut-chocolate side of the wrappers.
12. Top each with remaining wrappers, nut-chocolate side up.
13. Serve on a large platter garnished with mint sprigs or curled orange zest.
Note: Suzhou wrappers are thin, round wrappers used to make Asian dumplings, dim sum, wontons, etc. If you cannot find Suzzhou wrappers, substitute square wonton wrappers, but avoid potsticker skins because they are too thick for this recipe.
